homepage.heroSubline: Transforming Cook County homes since 2012. We specialize in bringing modern functionality to Chicago's historic bungalows and mid-century ranches.
aboutText: Founded during the recession by two Chicago carpenters, Apex Kitchen Remodel grew from fixing leaky bungalow kitchens to full-scale renovations. Today, we preserve the character of Chicago's historic homes while integrating smart storage solutions for modern living.

Our approach combines old-world craftsmanship with precision laser measurements. We account for Chicago's extreme temperature swings in every design - ensuring your new cabinetry won't warp when the polar vortex hits.
When we're not installing heated tile floors in Rogers Park or rebuilding vintage pantries in Oak Park, we're coaching youth hockey at McFetridge Sports Center.

valueProps.title: Chicago-Proof Materials
valueProps.description: We use thermally stable materials that withstand lake-effect humidity and subzero winters

services.name: Bungalow Kitchen Remodel
services.shortDescription: Preserving Chicago's historic charm while adding modern functionality.
services.description:
Chicago's iconic brick bungalows deserve kitchens that honor their craftsmanship while meeting today's needs. We specialize in sensitive updates for these 1920s gems - relocating bulky radiators to create work triangles, rebuilding original cabinetry with hidden organizers, and installing period-appropriate subway tile that withstands our freeze-thaw cycles.

Every project begins with documenting original details like built-in hutches or leaded glass cabinets. We then design around your home's unique quirks - maybe converting that awkward back porch into a breakfast nook, or adding a discreet coffee station where the icebox once stood.

Our Chicago-specific touches include vapor barriers behind walls to prevent condensation damage, and quarter-sawn oak thresholds that accommodate decades of foundation settling.
process: Step 1: Historic assessment and measurement of existing space
Step 2: Design presentation with 3D renderings showing preservation elements
Step 3: Careful demolition protecting original architectural features
Step 4: Installation with materials matched to your home's era

faq.question: How do you handle knob-and-tube wiring in old kitchens?
faq.answer: We partner with licensed electricians to safely replace outdated wiring while preserving original plaster walls. All work meets Chicago's strict historic district codes.

faq.question: Can you match my original cabinet doors?
faq.answer: Yes - we take door samples to our Humboldt Park woodshop to recreate profiles using the same old-growth lumber species.

faq.question: What flooring works best with radiant heat?
faq.answer: For bungalows, we recommend encaustic cement tiles or engineered hardwood - both stable enough for Chicago's temperature swings when paired with our subfloor systems.

services.name: Mid-Century Kitchen Update
services.shortDescription: Bringing 1950s-70s ranch kitchens into the 21st century.
services.description:
The sprawling galley kitchens of Chicago's postwar suburbs need smart updates for today's lifestyles. We reimagine these spaces by removing soffits to gain precious inches, replacing Formica with quartz that mimics period-appropriate patterns, and integrating appliances into sleek new cabinetry that nods to the home's atomic-age roots.

For Arlington Heights ranches and Schaumburg split-levels, we often convert unused formal dining areas into pantry extensions. Our specialty is designing hidden charging stations and appliance garages that maintain clean lines while adding functionality.

We address common mid-century issues like inadequate lighting by installing LED strips under cabinets (disguised as period-accurate aluminum trim) and upgrading those single-bulb ceiling fixtures to stylish pendant clusters.

Every project includes structural reinforcement for sagging floors - a common issue in these quickly-built suburban homes after decades of Chicago winters.
process: Step 1: Structural assessment of load-bearing walls and flooring
Step 2: Design consultation focusing on period-appropriate modernizations
Step 3: Soffit removal and space reconfiguration
Step 4: Installation of climate-adapted materials

faq.question: How do you modernize without losing the retro feel?
faq.answer: We source reproduction pulls from the era and use color palettes true to the period - think harvest gold or avocado green as accent colors rather than overwhelming themes.

faq.question: Can you work around my asbestos floor tiles?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Our certified team safely encapsulates or removes hazardous materials before beginning renovations, following all EPA guidelines.

faq.question: What's your solution for tiny postwar sinks?
faq.answer: We often salvage original steel sink bases and pair them with modern undermount stainless bowls - giving you more workspace while keeping the vintage aesthetic.

services.name: Luxury Condo Kitchens
services.shortDescription: High-end renovations for Chicago's downtown high-rises.
services.description:
Gold Coast and Streeterville condos demand kitchen designs as sophisticated as their skyline views. We specialize in high-rise renovations that navigate strict building boards and elevator logistics, transforming cookie-cutter spaces into chef-worthy showpieces.

Our premium packages include installing appliance garages that disappear into waterfall islands, integrating smart home technology without visible wiring, and creating custom ventilation solutions for high-floor units where traditional ducting isn't possible.

We work exclusively with local artisans - your marble countertops come from the same Bridgeport fabricator serving Chicago's Michelin-starred restaurants, while your glass backsplash is hand-cut in a Pilsen studio.

Every project includes detailed noise-reduction planning - sound-dampening underlayments prevent clattering pans from disturbing downstairs neighbors, and cabinet soft-close mechanisms meet even the most stringent condo association requirements.
process: Step 1: Building board approval coordination
Step 2: Precision laser measurements accounting for floor deflection
Step 3: Off-site fabrication to minimize onsite work time
Step 4: After-hours installation with building-approved freight elevators

faq.question: How do you handle deliveries in high-rises?
faq.answer: We schedule all material deliveries through building-approved freight elevators during designated hours, with protective floor coverings from lobby to unit.

faq.question: Can you match my building's existing millwork?
faq.answer: Our Lincoln Park woodshop can recreate any profile - we'll obtain a sample of your building's original specs for perfect matching.

faq.question: What's your solution for small condo kitchens?
faq.answer: We maximize every inch with appliance garages, counter-depth refrigeration, and multi-functional islands on locking casters for flexible layouts.

services.name: Accessible Kitchen Design
services.shortDescription: Creating functional kitchens for all ages and abilities.
services.description:
Chicago's aging housing stock often isn't designed for accessibility. We retrofit kitchens throughout Cook County with universal design principles - from grab-ready cabinet pulls in Lincoln Park two-flats to wheelchair-friendly galley layouts in Beverly ranches.

Our designs feature adjustable countertops on screw jacks (hidden behind period-appropriate trim), touch-activated faucets that work with arthritic hands, and smart storage that brings items within easy reach. For historic homes, we disguise accessibility features - a vintage-looking pie safe might conceal a slide-out step stool, or a depression-era bread box could hide medication organizers.

We pay special attention to lighting design, installing color-corrected LEDs under cabinets and inside drawers to help those with fading vision. All our accessible remodels include non-slip flooring options tested on Chicago's icy winters - like textured porcelain that mimics the look of vintage encaustic tiles.
process: Step 1: Needs assessment with occupational therapist input
Step 2: Preservation-conscious accessibility planning
Step 3: Custom fabrication of adaptive elements
Step 4: Installation with future adjustability in mind

faq.question: Can you make my vintage kitchen accessible without looking institutional?
faq.answer: Absolutely - we use Chicago-area craftsmen to create adaptive features that blend seamlessly with historic details, like carved wooden grab bars that match existing trim.

faq.question: What's the best flooring for wheelchair users in our climate?
faq.answer: We recommend European-style textured porcelain tiles - they provide traction when wet, won't crack in freeze-thaw cycles, and come in patterns matching Chicago's historic homes.

faq.question: How do you lower counters without compromising storage?
faq.answer: Our patented under-counter lift systems maintain full-depth storage while allowing height adjustment - great for multi-generational households.

services.name: Small Kitchen Solutions
services.shortDescription: Maximizing every inch in Chicago's compact kitchens.
services.description:
From Wicker Park studios to Lakeview two-flats, we excel at creating functional kitchens in tight spaces. Our designers employ custom solutions like appliance garages that tuck into chimney breasts, fold-down prep tables mounted on original brick walls, and slim-profile refrigeration disguised as cabinetry.

For Chicago's famous greystone buildings, we often convert awkward niches into coffee stations or baking centers. Walk-up apartments gain valuable storage through ceiling-height pull-down shelves and toe-kick drawers. We even design custom inserts for those peculiar triangular cabinets that plague vintage Chicago kitchens.

Every small kitchen remodel includes our patent-pending "Chicago corners" - specialized lazy susans that navigate the 135-degree angles common in old apartment layouts. We also install space-saving venting solutions, like downdraft ranges that preserve precious upper cabinet space in pre-war buildings.
process: Step 1: 3D laser scan of existing space
Step 2: Custom storage solution design
Step 3: Precision fabrication of space-saving elements
Step 4: Installation focusing on vertical storage

faq.question: How do you add counter space without expanding?
faq.answer: We install custom flip-up extensions on existing counters, butcher block covers for sinks, and narrow pull-out work surfaces between appliances.

faq.question: Can you make my tiny kitchen feel bigger?
faq.answer: Yes - strategically placed mirrors, glass-front upper cabinets, and continuous flooring materials create visual expansion. We also use lighting tricks to brighten dark Chicago kitchens.

faq.question: What's your solution for no pantry space?
faq.answer: We convert nearby closets into climate-controlled pantries with sliding shelves, or build floor-to-ceiling pantry cabinets as slim as 12 inches deep.

reviews.name: Marta K.
reviews.text: Apex transformed our 1916 bungalow kitchen without losing an inch of charm. They replicated our original cabinet doors exactly (right down to the wormholes!) while adding secret spice racks and a fridge that disappears behind period-appropriate panels. The heated tile floor has been a game-changer for our Chicago winters.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: Our 1962 Arlington Heights ranch kitchen went from dated to phenomenal. They removed the oppressive soffits, restored the original terrazzo floors we didn't know were under layers of linoleum, and designed a stunning atomic-age inspired backsplash. The new layout actually gives us more storage despite removing a wall.

reviews.name: Leila S.
reviews.text: Working within our high-rise's strict rules seemed impossible until Apex got involved. They fabricated everything off-site and installed it overnight to avoid building complaints. The custom walnut cabinetry hides every modern appliance imaginable, and the silent-close drawers don't disturb our downstairs neighbors.

reviews.name: Carlos D.
reviews.text: After my stroke, our old Logan Square kitchen became unusable. Apex created beautiful adaptations - countertops that lower at the touch of a button, cabinets with automatic lights inside, and a stove I can actually reach from my wheelchair. Everything looks perfectly normal when guests come over.

reviews.name: Yusef A.
reviews.text: Our Wicker Park studio kitchen went from non-functional to chef-worthy. They built a pull-out pantry in what was dead space next to the radiator, installed a counter that folds over the sink, and found an 18-inch dishwasher that fits perfectly. Now we can actually cook without tripping over each other.

reviews.name: Noemi P.
reviews.text: The team preserved all the original 1923 features of our Pilsen bungalow while adding modern conveniences. They converted the old icebox alcove into a coffee station, built a baking center where the pantry used to be, and even matched the historic Chicago common brick when relocating a doorway.

service-areas.title: Chicago Suburb Kitchen Remodeling Service Areas
service-areas.description: Serving Cook County communities including Naperville, Evanston, Schaumburg and more with climate-conscious kitchen remodels.

serviceAreas.content:
Apex Kitchen Remodel specializes in transforming kitchens in Naperville's upscale family homes, many built between the 1970s and 2000s. Our expertise includes modernizing outdated layouts, installing energy-efficient appliances, and creating custom cabinetry to withstand the harsh cold winters with heavy snow and ice. We understand the unique needs of Naperville homeowners, ensuring your kitchen remodel enhances both functionality and resale value.

From sleek contemporary designs to timeless traditional styles, we tailor our services to match Naperville's sophisticated aesthetic. Our team is familiar with local building codes and weather-resistant materials, ensuring your new kitchen is as durable as it is beautiful. Let us help you create the perfect gathering space for your family in this vibrant community.
serviceAreas.localContext: Naperville features upscale family homes primarily from the 1970s-2000s, requiring kitchen remodels that balance modern functionality with enduring style.

serviceAreas.content:
Serving Aurora's diverse housing stock from the 1960s through 2000s, Apex Kitchen Remodel brings fresh perspectives to every project. We specialize in adapting older kitchens to contemporary lifestyles while preserving their original charm. Our solutions account for Aurora's harsh winter climate, incorporating durable materials and proper insulation to keep your kitchen comfortable year-round.

Whether you're in a mid-century ranch or a newer subdivision home, we create kitchens that reflect Aurora's eclectic character. From space-saving solutions for smaller homes to luxury upgrades for larger properties, our designs always prioritize functionality and flow. We're proud to help Aurora residents love their kitchens again.
serviceAreas.localContext: Aurora's housing ranges from 1960s-2000s developments, presenting unique remodeling challenges and opportunities in each neighborhood.

serviceAreas.content:
Apex Kitchen Remodel excels at updating Schaumburg's 1970s-80s suburban kitchens to meet today's standards. We understand how these original layouts often lack modern conveniences and storage solutions. Our designs incorporate smart space utilization while respecting the architectural integrity of Schaumburg's classic suburban homes.

Considering Schaumburg's harsh winters, we recommend materials that withstand temperature fluctuations and moisture. From cabinet refacing to complete gut renovations, we help Schaumburg homeowners create kitchens that are both stylish and practical for family life. Our local expertise ensures your remodel will stand the test of time in this established community.
serviceAreas.localContext: Schaumburg's 1970s-80s suburban developments often feature kitchens needing functional updates while maintaining their original character.

serviceAreas.content:

For Evanston's harsh winters, we recommend insulation upgrades and durable materials that complement your home's original features. Whether you need a subtle refresh or a complete transformation, we create kitchens that honor Evanston's rich architectural heritage while meeting your family's needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Evanston's historic homes near Northwestern University require kitchen remodels that respect their architectural significance.

serviceAreas.content:

From galley kitchen expansions to creating modern open-concept spaces, we help Arlington Heights homeowners reimagine their living areas. Our designs respect the neighborhood's character while introducing contemporary conveniences that make daily life easier and more enjoyable.

From preserving original hardwood floors to creating butler's pantries that nod to Wheaton's history, we help homeowners achieve timeless designs. Whether you're in a Victorian-era home or a mid-century ranch, we'll create a kitchen that serves your family beautifully for years to come.
